_description: Hand-curated arb-pair fixtures for T78 match-quality audit. 'bad' pairs MUST be rejected (score < 0.4 or hard-reject) by the current T76+T77 matcher. 'good' pairs MUST score > 0.7. Used by api/tests/test_arb_match_quality.py. Each 'bad' entry includes an 'expected_reason' aligned with T77's REJECT_REASONS. The 'known_t77_gaps' section lists cases the user-flagged-FP catalog includes that current T77 does NOT yet catch — these are tracked as xfail in the test suite so T78 surfaces them without blocking CI.

bad.poly_title: Will Bitcoin trade above $80,000 by Dec 31 2025?
bad.kalshi_title: Will Bitcoin trade above $90,000 by Dec 31 2025?
bad.notes: Same asset and window but different price threshold — caught by T77 threshold-mismatch.

bad.poly_title: Will Democrats win the US Senate majority in 2026?
bad.kalshi_title: Will Democrats win the Florida State Senate majority in 2026?
bad.notes: US Senate vs Florida State Senate — caught by T77 jurisdiction logic.

bad.poly_title: Will Eric Adams win the New York mayoral race in 2025?
bad.kalshi_title: Will Karen Bass win the Florida mayoral race in 2026?

bad.poly_title: Will the US enter a recession in 2025?
bad.kalshi_title: Will the US enter a recession in 2027?
bad.notes: Same event, non-overlapping in-year windows — T77 rejects.

bad.poly_title: Will the Democratic candidate win the Illinois US House race in 2026?
bad.kalshi_title: Will the Democratic candidate win the Florida US House race in 2026?
bad.notes: Different states — T77 jurisdiction logic catches state-level conflicts.
Same asset/threshold but non-overlapping 'in YEAR' anchors — T76 anchors full Jan–Dec window so T77 rejects on window non-overlap.

bad.poly_title: Will Jeff Hurd win the Nevada Republican primary in 2026?
bad.kalshi_title: Will Jeff Hurd win the New York Republican primary in 2026?
bad.notes: Same candidate name but different states — must not match.

bad.poly_title: Will the Democratic candidate win the Texas gubernatorial race in 2026?
bad.kalshi_title: Will the Democratic candidate win the California gubernatorial race in 2026?

bad.poly_title: Will the US enter a recession in 2025?
bad.kalshi_title: Will the US enter a recession in 2028?

bad.notes: Same threshold but non-overlapping resolution years.

good.poly_title: Will Donald Trump win the US presidential election by Nov 5 2024?
good.kalshi_title: Will Donald J. Trump win the US presidential election by Nov 5 2024?
good.notes: Same event, slight name format diff.
Same threshold, same window, identical wording — sanity check baseline.

good.poly_title: Will the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl by Feb 8 2026?
good.kalshi_title: Will the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl by Feb 8 2026?
good.notes: Same game; identical resolution date and entity.

good.poly_title: Will Republicans control the US Senate by Nov 3 2026?
good.kalshi_title: Will Republicans hold the US Senate majority by Nov 3 2026?
good.notes: Same chamber, same election date, slight wording variation.

known_t77_gaps.poly_title: Will Donald Trump win the US presidential election by Nov 5 2024?
known_t77_gaps.kalshi_title: Will Donald Trump win the US presidential election by Nov 7 2028?
known_t77_gaps.gap_explanation: T76 returns windows with earliest=None, latest=date — T77's windows_overlap() treats half-open intervals as overlapping, so 'by 2024' and 'by 2028' pass the hard-reject check. Fix: T76 should anchor earliest to the same year/month as latest when only one anchor is found, or T77 should require strict latest-date proximity (e.g. |latest_a - latest_b| < 90d) for events without an explicit earliest.

known_t77_gaps.poly_title: Will the Fed cut rates at the FOMC meeting by March 18 2026?
known_t77_gaps.kalshi_title: Will the Fed cut rates at the FOMC meeting by June 17 2026?
known_t77_gaps.gap_explanation: Same half-open-window issue; meeting-specific calendar events need a tighter date-proximity check.

known_t77_gaps.poly_title: Will the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl by Feb 9 2025?
known_t77_gaps.kalshi_title: Will the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl by Feb 8 2026?
known_t77_gaps.gap_explanation: Same half-open-window issue; sports-final events resolve on a single day, requiring strict latest-equality.

known_t77_gaps.poly_title: Will the Ethereum Merge upgrade complete by Dec 31 2022?
known_t77_gaps.kalshi_title: Will the Ethereum Pectra upgrade complete by Dec 31 2026?
known_t77_gaps.gap_explanation: Half-open windows + 4-year gap not caught; relies on entity disambiguation (Merge vs Pectra) which T77's tokenizer treats as both crypto-topic.

known_t77_gaps.poly_title: Will Oppenheimer win Best Picture at the Academy Awards by Mar 11 2024?
known_t77_gaps.kalshi_title: Will Anora win Best Picture at the Academy Awards by Mar 3 2025?
known_t77_gaps.gap_explanation: Half-open windows; entity match on 'Best Picture / Academy Awards' overrides the year diff.
